214	export const resolveWordSpellConfig = (
215	  saved: Record<string, unknown> | null,
216	): WordSpellConfig => {
217	  const mode =
218	    (saved as { mode?: unknown } | null)?.mode === 'picture'
219	      ? 'picture'
220	      : 'recall';
221	  const base =
222	    mode === 'picture' ? DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG : DEFAULT_RECALL_CONFIG;
223	  const baseClone: WordSpellConfig = {
224	    ...base,
225	    rounds: base.rounds
226	      ? base.rounds.map((r) => ({ ...r }))
227	      : undefined,
228	    source: base.source
229	      ? { ...base.source, filter: { ...base.source.filter } }
230	      : undefined,
231	  };
232	
233	  if (!saved || saved.component !== 'WordSpell') return baseClone;
234	
235	  // Simple-mode: delegate to word-spell's library-source resolver.
236	  // Any config with selectedUnits (but not explicitly advanced) was produced
237	  // by the picker and must be re-resolved so source.filter stays in sync.
238	  if (
239	    saved.configMode === 'simple' ||
240	    (saved.configMode !== 'advanced' &&
241	      Array.isArray(saved.selectedUnits))
242	  ) {
243	    const savedInput = saved.inputMethod;
244	    return resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig({
245	      configMode: 'simple',
246	      ...saved,
247	      inputMethod:
248	        savedInput === 'type' || savedInput === 'both'
249	          ? savedInput
250	          : 'drag',
251	    } as Parameters<typeof resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig>[0]);
252	  }
253	
254	  // Advanced merge — start from mode-appropriate base, then layer saved
255	  // fields, then enforce the mode invariant.
256	  const merged: WordSpellConfig = {
257	    ...baseClone,
258	    ...(saved as Partial<WordSpellConfig>),
259	    gameId: 'word-spell',
260	    component: 'WordSpell',
261	  };
262	
263	  // When the picker set selectedUnits, re-derive source.filter so
264	  // the filter stays in sync even in advanced mode.
265	  if (Array.isArray(merged.selectedUnits) && merged.source) {
266	    const derived = resolveWordSpellSimpleConfig({
267	      configMode: 'simple',
268	      selectedUnits: merged.selectedUnits,
269	      region: merged.source.filter.region,
270	      inputMethod: merged.inputMethod,
271	    });
272	    merged.source = derived.source;
273	  }
274	
275	  // Mode invariant:
276	  // recall  ⇒ source defined ∧ rounds undefined
277	  // picture ⇒ rounds defined ∧ source undefined
278	  if (merged.mode === 'recall') {
279	    if (merged.rounds) {
280	      const { rounds: _ignored, ...rest } = merged;
281	      return rest as WordSpellConfig;
282	    }
283	    return merged;
284	  }
285	
286	  // mode === 'picture' (or 'sentence-gap' — treat as picture for default fallback)
287	  const picture = { ...merged };
288	  if (picture.source) {
289	    delete picture.source;
290	  }
291	  if (!Array.isArray(picture.rounds) || picture.rounds.length === 0) {
292	    picture.rounds = sliceWordSpellRounds(8);
293	  }
294	  picture.totalRounds = Math.min(
295	    picture.rounds.length,
296	    WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL.length,
297	  );
298	  return picture;
299	};

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji/docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md`

```text
1	---
2	date: 2026-05-01
3	topic: consolidate-word-emoji
4	---
5	
6	# Consolidate Word and Emoji Data
7	
8	## Summary
9	
10	Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields directly into curriculum JSON entries so
11	that any word in the library can carry a visual. Picture mode switches from a
12	hardcoded 8-word pool to library-sourced rounds filtered by visual availability.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## Problem Frame
17	
18	WordSpell's picture mode shows an emoji or image alongside each word, but the
19	visual data lives in a hardcoded array (`WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL`) of 8 words in
20	the route loader — completely disconnected from the curriculum word library. This
21	means picture mode is locked to those 8 words regardless of what the teacher
22	configures, and adding new picture-mode words requires a code change rather than
23	a data edit.
24	
25	The word library already powers recall mode with rich filtering (region, level,
26	graphemes, phonemes), but none of that infrastructure is available to picture
27	mode because the visual data isn't part of the word data.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## Requirements
32	
33	**Data structure**
34	
35	- R1. `CurriculumEntry` gains optional `emoji?: string` and `image?: string`
36	  fields. Both are optional — most words will have neither.
37	- R2. `WordHit` gains matching optional `emoji?` and `image?` fields, populated
38	  from the curriculum entry during filtering. Specifically, `joinHits` in
39	  `filter.ts` must propagate `emoji` and `image` from `CurriculumEntry` into
40	  the constructed `WordHit`, and `draftToHit` must propagate them from
41	  `DraftEntry`.
42	- R3. `DraftEntry` gains optional `emoji?` and `image?` fields for authoring
43	  parity.
44	- R4. The `toWordSpellRound` adapter maps `emoji` and `image` from `WordHit`
45	  through to `WordSpellRound`.
46	
47	**Curriculum data**
48	
49	- R5. The 7 existing hardcoded emoji words that already have curriculum entries
50	  (ant, can, cat, dog, pin, sad, sun) gain an `emoji` field in their respective
51	  `aus/level1.json` or `aus/level2.json` curriculum entries.
52	- R6. "mum" is added as a new `CurriculumEntry` in `aus/level2.json` (level 2
53	  covers graphemes `m` and `u`) with its emoji `🤱`. A corresponding `WordCore`
54	  entry must also be added to `core/level2.json` (with `word` and
55	  `syllableCount`) — without it, `joinHits` will silently discard the
56	  curriculum entry.
57	
58	**Filtering**
59	
60	- R7. `WordFilter` gains a `hasVisual?: boolean` option. When `true`,
61	  `filterWords` returns only words where `emoji` or `image` is set.
62	
63	**Picture mode behavior**
64	
65	- R8. When WordSpell is in picture mode, the config resolver uses
66	  library-sourced rounds with `hasVisual: true` in the filter, instead of
67	  falling back to the hardcoded pool. This requires inverting the existing mode
68	  invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` (which currently deletes `source` for
69	  picture mode) so that picture mode becomes `source` defined with
70	  `hasVisual: true`. The existing test asserting source is dropped for picture
71	  mode must be updated to assert the new behavior. If a saved config has
72	  explicit `rounds` with emoji/image data, those are honored as-is (no library
73	  fetch). If `hasVisual` filtering returns zero results, the game falls back to
74	  recall mode (audio-only) rather than displaying a blank screen.
75	- R9. The hardcoded `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` and `sliceWordSpellRounds` are
76	  removed.
77	
78	---
79	
80	## Acceptance Examples
81	
82	- AE1. **Covers R4, R5, R8.** Given a WordSpell game in picture mode with
83	  default config, when the game loads, it pulls rounds from `aus/level2.json`
84	  (and `level1.json`) curriculum entries that have an `emoji` field set, and the
85	  emoji is rendered via `EmojiQuestion`.
86	- AE2. **Covers R7.** Given a `WordFilter` with `hasVisual: true` and
87	  `region: 'aus'`, when `filterWords` runs, only entries with a non-empty
88	  `emoji` or `image` field are returned.
89	- AE3. **Covers R1, R2.** Given a curriculum entry without `emoji` or `image`,
90	  when it appears in a `WordHit`, `emoji` and `image` are `undefined` and the
91	  word works normally in recall mode.
92	
93	---
94	
95	## Success Criteria
96	
97	- Picture mode loads rounds from the word library instead of a hardcoded array.
98	- Adding a new picture-mode word requires only a data edit (adding `emoji` or
99	  `image` to a curriculum JSON entry), not a code change.
100	- Existing recall mode and sentence-gap mode are unaffected.
101	
102	---
103	
104	## Scope Boundaries
105	
106	- Tagging additional words beyond the existing 8 with emojis
107	- Emoji/image editing UI in the AuthoringPanel
108	- Emoji display in WordLibraryExplorer cards
109	- Image upload or asset management
110	- Multi-region emoji support (only `aus` curriculum entries are tagged in this
111	  pass)
112	
113	---
114	
115	## Key Decisions
116	
117	- **Embed in curriculum JSON, not a separate lookup:** single source of truth per
118	  word, no join at query time. Trades separation of concerns for simplicity.
119	- **Both emoji and image fields:** `WordSpellRound` already supports both; the
120	  curriculum entry should mirror that capability even though only emoji is used
121	  today.
122	- **Auto-filter, not configurable:** picture mode always filters to words with
123	  visuals. No teacher toggle for text-only fallback in picture mode.
124	- **No IndexedDB migration:** all new fields are optional. Existing drafts and
125	  saved configs remain valid without a schema version bump.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## Outstanding Questions
130	
131	### Deferred to Planning
132	
133	- Affects R6 · Needs research — What are the correct IPA transcription and
134	  grapheme mappings for "mum" in `aus` region?
135	- Affects R8 · Technical — Should the default picture-mode `WordFilter` constrain
136	  to specific levels/graphemes, or pull from all levels that have visuals?
137
```
</details></tool-use>

import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
import {
  ALL_REGIONS,
  GRAPHEMES_BY_LEVEL,
  cumulativeGraphemes,
} from './levels';
import type { CurriculumEntry, Region } from './types';

const curriculumChunks = import.meta.glob<{
  default: CurriculumEntry[];
}>('./curriculum/*/level*.json', { eager: true });

const filesByRegion = new Map<Region, Map<number, CurriculumEntry[]>>();
for (const [path, mod] of Object.entries(curriculumChunks)) {
  const match = /curriculum\/(\w+)\/level(\d+)\.json$/.exec(path);
  if (!match) continue;
  const region = match[1] as Region;
  if (!ALL_REGIONS.includes(region)) continue;
  const level = Number(match[2]);
  if (!filesByRegion.has(region)) filesByRegion.set(region, new Map());
  filesByRegion.get(region)!.set(level, mod.default);
}

const regionsWithData: Region[] = [...filesByRegion.keys()].filter(
  (r) => (filesByRegion.get(r)?.size ?? 0) > 0,
);

const usedAt = (region: Region, level: number): Set<string> => {
  const out = new Set<string>();
  const entries = filesByRegion.get(region)?.get(level) ?? [];
  for (const e of entries) {
    for (const g of e.graphemes) {
      out.add(`${g.g}|${g.p}`);
    }
  }
  return out;
};

const cumulativeKeys = (level: number): Set<string> => {
  const out = new Set<string>();
  for (const u of cumulativeGraphemes(level)) out.add(`${u.g}|${u.p}`);
  return out;
};

const newlyDeclaredAt = (level: number): Set<string> => {
  const cur = cumulativeKeys(level);
  const prev = cumulativeKeys(level - 1);
  const out = new Set<string>();
  for (const k of cur) if (!prev.has(k)) out.add(k);
  return out;
};

describe('levels vs data invariant: every (g,p) used at L_n is in cumulative declared L1..L_n', () => {
  for (const region of regionsWithData) {
    const levels = [...filesByRegion.get(region)!.keys()].toSorted(
      (a, b) => a - b,
    );
    for (const level of levels) {
      it(`${region} L${level}: used pairs are all declared by this level`, () => {
        const used = usedAt(region, level);
```

Important finding. The mode detection at line 217-220 only checks for `'picture'` -- anything else (including `'sentence-gap'`) falls through to `'recall'`. But the comment at line 286 says sentence-gap is treated as picture. Let me trace this logic carefully.

Currently: if `saved.mode === 'picture'`, `mode` is set to `'picture'`. Otherwise `'recall'`. So `sentence-gap` saved mode gets resolved as `recall` mode in the base selection, but then the merge at line 256 applies `...(saved as Partial<WordSpellConfig>)` which sets `merged.mode` back to `'sentence-gap'`. 1	import type { WordHit } from './types';
2	import { seededRandom } from '@/lib/seeded-random';
3	
4	/**
5	 * Returns up to `limit` hits chosen uniformly at random from `hits`.
6	 * With a `seed`, the output is deterministic — same inputs give the
7	 * same sequence. Without a seed, uses `Math.random` so callers get
8	 * fresh samples on every call.
9	 *
10	 * Implementation: Fisher–Yates shuffle on a copy of `hits`, then slice.
11	 * Never mutates the input array.
12	 */
13	export const sampleHits = (
14	  hits: WordHit[],
15	  limit: number,
16	  seed?: string,
17	): WordHit[] => {
18	  if (hits.length === 0 || limit <= 0) return [];
19	  const random = seed === undefined ? Math.random : seededRandom(seed);
20	  const copy = [...hits];
21	  for (let i = copy.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
22	    const j = Math.floor(random() * (i + 1));
23	    [copy[i], copy[j]] = [copy[j]!, copy[i]!];
24	  }
25	  return copy.slice(0, Math.min(limit, copy.length));
26	};
27

This is a real issue. The plan adds `hasVisual` to `filterSignature` in Task 2, which changes the signature format. Two existing tests in `useLibraryRounds.test.tsx` at lines 161 and 183 hardcode the old signature format. These tests will fail after Task 2 is applied, but the plan never mentions updating them. Task 8 only adds new tests to `useLibraryRounds.test.tsx`.

Let me also check one more concern -- what happens when `sentence-gap` mode hits the new picture-mode branch. Sentence-gap has its own round structure with `sentence`, `gaps`, etc. that differs from picture mode's emoji-based rounds. The new code would inject `hasVisual: true` into the source filter for sentence-gap mode too, which would restrict sentence-gap to only words with emojis/images.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 06:45:04Z)**_



**Finding 3/5 — MEDIUM: totalRounds no longer capped for picture mode**

The old code hardcoded `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` (8 words) and sliced it, implicitly capping picture mode at 8 rounds. The new plan removes that pool and uses library-sourced rounds, but doesn't add an explicit cap. If a saved config has `totalRounds: 50` and only 8 words have visuals, `pickWithRecycling` will recycle heavily — the student sees the same 8 words repeated ~6 times in one session.

This isn't a bug per se — recall mode already works this way with small word pools — but it's a behavioral change from the implicit 8-round cap that picture mode had before.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 06:46:34Z)**_

<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**totalRounds is no longer implicitly capped at 8 for picture mode. With only 8 visual words, high totalRounds values cause heavy recycling. How should we handle this?**

Options:
- **Skip (Recommended)** - Accept the behavioral change. Recall mode already recycles with small pools — picture mode can too. The requirements doc deferred totalRounds truncation to SRS.
- **Add cap to plan** - Add a step capping totalRounds to the number of available visual words (e.g., min(totalRounds, hits.length)). Prevents recycling but limits session length.
- **Defer to Open Questions** - Add this as an open question in the plan for the executor to decide during implementation.


**Answer:** Skip (Recommended)
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 06:46:44Z)**_

Skipped — consistent with the requirements doc deferring totalRounds truncation to SRS.

**Finding 4/5 — MEDIUM: Fallback uses original filter signature for seen-words**

In Task 8, when `hasVisual: true` returns zero results, the plan retries without `hasVisual`. But the `filterSignature` still includes `hasVisual=true` from the original filter. This means the seen-words dedup key (used by `pickWithRecycling`) was computed with the hasVisual filter, but the actual word pool comes from the non-hasVisual query. The mismatch means:

- Words seen in a previous hasVisual session won't be deduplicated against this fallback pool
- The recycling tracker thinks it's a different word set than what's actually being served

In practice this is low-impact because fallback only fires when there are zero visual words (an edge case of an edge case), and the worst outcome is suboptimal word ordering, not incorrect behavior.

**Finding 5/5 — LOW: Region hardcoded to 'aus' in picture-mode default**

The `DEFAULT_PICTURE_CONFIG` and the fallback source in `resolveWordSpellConfig` both hardcode `region: 'aus'`. This mirrors the existing behavior (the hardcoded pool was also aus-only), but the requirements doc's success criterion says "adding a new picture-mode word requires only a data edit" — yet adding emoji words to a non-aus region would also require a code change to the default filter.

**The default picture-mode filter hardcodes region: 'aus'. This mirrors existing behavior but means adding emoji words for other regions requires a code change, not just a data edit. How should we handle this?**

Options:

- **Note in plan** - Add a comment in the plan noting this is a known limitation, so the executor documents it for future work.
- **Defer to Open Questions** - Add this as an open question in the plan for future consideration.


**Answer:** Note in plan

1	# Consolidate Word and Emoji Data Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4	
5	**Goal:** Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields into curriculum JSON so picture mode uses library-sourced rounds instead of a hardcoded 8-word pool.
6	
7	**Architecture:** Add `emoji?` and `image?` to the type chain (`CurriculumEntry` → `WordHit` → `WordSpellRound`), add `hasVisual` filtering, tag 7 existing curriculum entries with emojis and add "mum" as the 8th entry, then invert the picture-mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` to use a library source with `hasVisual: true` instead of a hardcoded round pool. Remove the hardcoded pool entirely.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Vitest, React (hook tests via `@testing-library/react`)
10	
11	**Spec:** `docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md`

1053	
1054	- **"mum" IPA/graphemes:** `/mʌm/` with graphemes `m/m`, `u/ʌ`, `m/m`. This follows the same pattern as existing level 2 words with the `ʌ` vowel (e.g. "mug" = `m/m`, `u/ʌ`, `g/g`).
1055	- **Default picture-mode filter scope:** Pull from all levels with visuals (no level/grapheme constraint). The default `WordFilter` uses `region: 'aus'` and `hasVisual: true` with no level restriction, so any curriculum word tagged with emoji or image across any level is included.

```text
1	# Handoff: Consolidate Word and Emoji Data (#277)
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-05-01
4	**Branch:** issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji
5	**Worktree:** worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji
6	**Worktree path:** /Users/leocaseiro/Sites/base-skill/worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji
7	**Git status:** clean
8	**Last commit:** f02b8f9d docs(brainstorm): apply review findings to consolidate-word-emoji spec (#277)
9	**PR:** #281 — open
10	
11	## Resume command
12	
13	```
14	/resync
15	cd worktrees/issue-277-consolidate-word-emoji
16	# Execute implementation plan based on the requirements doc
17	```
18	
19	## Current state
20	
21	**Task:** Embed optional `emoji` and `image` fields into curriculum JSON so picture mode uses library-sourced rounds instead of a hardcoded 8-word pool.
22	**Phase:** planning
23	**Progress:** Requirements doc complete and reviewed. Next step is `ce-plan` to produce an implementation plan.
24	
25	## What we did
26	
27	Brainstormed and wrote a requirements document covering 9 requirements (R1-R9) for consolidating word and emoji data. Ran `ce-doc-review` with 5 reviewer personas, applied 6 findings, and deferred 1 (totalRounds silent truncation — SRS will address this later). Supersedes issue #266 with a comment linking it to #277.
28	
29	## Decisions made
30	
31	- **Embed in curriculum JSON, not a separate lookup** — single source of truth per word, no join at query time
32	- **Both emoji and image fields** — `WordSpellRound` already supports both; curriculum should mirror that capability
33	- **Auto-filter with `hasVisual: true`** — picture mode always filters to words with visuals, no teacher toggle
34	- **No IndexedDB migration needed** — all new fields are optional, existing drafts/configs remain valid
35	- **"mum" goes in level 2** — level 2 covers graphemes `m` and `u`; needs both `CurriculumEntry` in `aus/level2.json` and `WordCore` in `core/level2.json`
36	- **Zero-results fallback to recall mode** — if `hasVisual` filtering returns nothing, fall back to audio-only rather than blank screen
37	- **Defer totalRounds silent truncation** — SRS for rounds is coming soon, making this moot
38	
39	## Spec / Plan
40	
41	- docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md
42	
43	## Key files
44	
45	- `src/data/words/types.ts` — `CurriculumEntry`, `WordHit`, `DraftEntry`, `WordFilter` types need emoji/image fields (R1-R3, R7)
46	- `src/data/words/filter.ts:173-189` — `joinHits` must propagate emoji/image from curriculum to `WordHit` (R2)
47	- `src/data/words/filter.ts` — `draftToHit` must propagate emoji/image from `DraftEntry` (R2)
48	- `src/data/words/adapters.ts` — `toWordSpellRound` must map emoji/image through (R4)
49	- `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level1.json` — add emoji to ant, pin (R5)
50	- `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level2.json` — add emoji to can, cat, dog, sad, sun; add mum entry (R5, R6)
51	- `src/data/words/core/level2.json` — add mum `WordCore` entry (R6)
52	- `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:83-92` — `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` to be removed (R9)
53	- `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:286-298` — picture mode invariant must be inverted to use `source` with `hasVisual: true` (R8)
54	- `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts` — already maps via `toWordSpellRound`, will pick up emoji/image automatically
55	
56	## Open questions / blockers
57	
58	- [ ] What are the correct IPA transcription and grapheme mappings for "mum" in `aus` region? (affects R6, needs research during planning)
59	- [ ] Should the default picture-mode `WordFilter` constrain to specific levels/graphemes, or pull from all levels that have visuals? (affects R8)
60	
61	## Next steps
62	
63	1. [ ] Review and merge PR #281 (requirements doc)
64	2. [ ] Run `ce-plan` to produce an implementation plan from the requirements
65	3. [ ] Execute the plan using TDD (tests first for each requirement)
66	
67	## Context to remember
68	
69	- `joinHits` joins curriculum entries against core entries by word — if a `WordCore` entry is missing, the curriculum entry is silently discarded. "mum" must be added to both `aus/level2.json` AND `core/level2.json`.
70	- The mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` currently _deletes_ `source` for picture mode — it must be _inverted_ so picture mode becomes source-defined with `hasVisual: true`.
71	- `WordSpell.tsx` already renders emoji/image from rounds correctly — no changes needed there.
72	- Issue #266 was superseded by #277 — a comment was added to #266 linking to this work.

Always print the full path (including worktree directory) when referencing files in output.

**Why:** Relative paths like `docs/superpowers/plans/foo.md` are ambiguous in a worktree setup — the user can't tell whether the file is in the main repo or which worktree it lives in, making it hard to navigate to.

9	# Git workflow (all agents and models)

11	Full policy: [CLAUDE.md](../../CLAUDE.md). This file repeats the **non-negotiable steps** so Cursor and other tools behave the same as Claude Code.

13	## Hard gate before editing tracked files
14	
15	Do **not** create, modify, delete, or run formatters against project source from the **main repository checkout** (the clone whose workspace root is `.../base-skill` and **not** under `.../base-skill/worktrees/<name>/`).

21	3. If you are on the main tree and the user did **not** explicitly say to work in the root checkout (e.g. “hotfix on main”, “skip worktree”): **stop and create a worktree first** — do not patch `src/`, config, or docs in the root.

23	## Starting work (mandatory sequence)
24	
25	From the **repository root** (main checkout), not from inside an existing worktree:
26	
27	```bash
28	git fetch origin master
29	git worktree add ./worktrees/<short-name> -b <branch-name> origin/master
30	cd ./worktrees/<short-name>

33

35	
36	Verify the directory is ignored before relying on `./worktrees/`:
37	
38	```bash

40	```
41	
42	## Rules
43	
44	- **Never commit directly to `master`.**
45	- **Every task** (code, tests, Storybook, **plans, specs, markdown docs**) goes through a branch in a worktree unless the user opts out in the message.
46	- Open **Cursor’s workspace folder** as `./worktrees/<name>` when possible so tools default to the correct tree.
47	- After merge to `master`, remove the worktree: `git worktree remove ./worktrees/<name>`.

49	## Commits (baby steps)
50	
51	- When a logical unit of work is **done and good** (tests pass, lint clean for touched files), **commit** before starting the next unit.
52	- **One PR may contain many commits** — that is fine. Smaller commits make review, `git cherry-pick`, and targeted `git revert` easier.

# Handoff

Adapted from the [Cult of Claude handoff skill](https://cultofclaude.com/skills/handoff/) ([source](https://github.com/robertguss/claude-code-toolkit/blob/main/skills/handoff/SKILL.md)), tailored for BaseSkill's worktree + `/resync` + baby-step commit workflow.

## When to use

- Just saved a plan or spec (→ next session executes it)
- Just raised or merged a PR (→ next session starts follow-up work)
- Ending a work session or context-switching
- User says "hand this off", "continue in a new session", "pick this up later"

Explicit invocation: `/handoff` or `/continue`.

## Worktree rule

Handoffs are written inside the **active worktree**, never on `master`. The
worktree must live at `<project-root>/worktrees/<name>/` — **not** inside
`.claude/worktrees/`.

### Why this matters

`.claude/` is a hardcoded sensitive directory in Claude Code. Every file
write inside it (including `.claude/worktrees/<anything>/...`) triggers a
permission prompt that **no `permissions.allow` rule can override**. A
handoff written inside `.claude/worktrees/<name>/.claude/handoffs/` will
prompt on every write — `mkdir`, the Markdown file itself, and the commit.

### Before writing the handoff, check `pwd`

| Current directory                  | Action                                                                                     |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `<root>/worktrees/<name>/`         | ✅ proceed                                                                                 |
| `<root>` (master)                  | ❌ stop; ask user to create a worktree (see below)                                         |
| `<root>/.claude/worktrees/<name>/` | ❌ stop; tell the user this path is broken (see "Recovering" below) and ask how to proceed |

### Creating a worktree

Use Bash — **never the `EnterWorktree` tool**:

```bash
git worktree add ./worktrees/<branch-name> -b <branch-name>
cd ./worktrees/<branch-name>
```

`EnterWorktree` hardcodes the base path to `.claude/worktrees/<random-slug>/`
and is blocked by a `PreToolUse` hook in `.claude/settings.json`. If the hook
ever misfires, refuse the tool anyway and use the Bash recipe above.

The `using-git-worktrees` skill follows this convention automatically.

### Recovering from a `.claude/worktrees/` worktree

If `pwd` is already inside `.claude/worktrees/<name>/`, do **not** write the
handoff there. Surface this to the user:

> "I'm in `.claude/worktrees/<name>/`, which triggers a permission prompt on
> every file write. Two options: (a) move it with
> `git worktree move .claude/worktrees/<name> worktrees/<branch>` then `cd`
> over and continue, or (b) leave the existing worktree alone and create a
> fresh one at `worktrees/<branch>` for the new work. Which do you prefer?"

Wait for the user's choice before writing.

## Process

### Step 1 — Auto-detect context

Run these commands and keep the output for the template:

```bash
git branch --show-current
git worktree list | grep "$(pwd)"
git status --porcelain | wc -l
git rev-list --count @{u}..HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo 0
git log -1 --pretty=format:"%h %s"
gh pr view --json number,state,url 2>/dev/null || true
```

Also scan `docs/superpowers/specs/` and `docs/superpowers/plans/` for recently-modified files whose slug matches the current branch or task (optional — include only if clearly relevant).

### Step 2 — Ask one question

> "Anything specific you want captured? (key decisions, gotchas, things you'll forget)"

Single prompt, no follow-up loop. The user can answer briefly or skip.

### Step 3 — Generate the document

Fill in this template. **Omit any section with no content** — keep the document scannable.

````markdown
# Handoff: <brief description>

**Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
**Branch:** <branch-name>
**Worktree:** worktrees/<name>
**Worktree path:** <absolute path>
**Git status:** clean | N uncommitted | N unpushed commits ahead of origin
**Last commit:** <sha> <subject>
**PR:** #NNN — open|merged|closed (omit if none)

## Resume command

```
/resync
cd worktrees/<name>
<next action — e.g., execute plan docs/…md, open PR, resume debugging>
```

## Current state

**Task:** <what we're working on>
**Phase:** exploration | planning | implementation | debugging | review | shipped
**Progress:** <milestone or %>

## What we did

<2–3 sentences>

## Decisions made

- **<decision>** — <why>

## Spec / Plan

- docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md
- docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md

## Key files

- src/…/foo.ts:42 — <what and why>

## Open questions / blockers

- [ ] <question>

## Next steps

1. [ ] <first action>
2. [ ] <second>

## Context to remember

<constraints, user preferences, domain knowledge not obvious from the code>
````

### Step 4 — Write the file

Save to `.claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<brief-slug>.md`. Slug is 2–5 lowercase words joined by hyphens, matching the branch topic.

### Step 5 — Commit as a baby step

```bash
git add .claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md
git commit -m "docs(handoff): <brief description>"
```

### Step 6 — Print the resume hint

Tell the user, using the **absolute path** (run `pwd` if needed and join with the handoff filename — never print the repo-relative form):

> Saved handoff: `/absolute/path/to/.claude/handoffs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.md` — paste that path in a new session to resume.

## Quality check (before writing)

Mental pass — no validation script:

1. Could a fresh Claude (or Cursor) pick up from this?
2. Are decisions traceable (the "why", not just the "what")?
3. Are next steps actionable and specific?
4. Are file paths anchored (`foo.ts:42`, not "that function")?

If any answer is "no", tighten the relevant section before saving.

## What to capture

### Always

- Decisions with reasoning — the "why"
- File paths touched, with intent
- Current progress — where we stopped
- Actionable next steps
- User context — constraints, preferences, domain knowledge

### When relevant

- Errors hit and how they resolved (or didn't)
- Dead ends — approaches tried that didn't work (saves re-exploration)

### Skip

- Verbose tool output
- Intermediate reasoning that reached conclusions
- Information obvious from the code

25	## What we did
26	
27	Brainstormed and wrote a requirements document covering 9 requirements (R1-R9) for consolidating word and emoji data. Ran `ce-doc-review` on both the requirements doc and the implementation plan. Supersedes issue #266 with a comment linking it to #277. Implementation plan written with 9 TDD tasks, reviewed with 5 reviewer personas (5 findings resolved: 2 fixed, 2 skipped, 1 noted as known limitation).
28	
29	## Decisions made
30	
31	- **Embed in curriculum JSON, not a separate lookup** — single source of truth per word, no join at query time
32	- **Both emoji and image fields** — `WordSpellRound` already supports both; curriculum should mirror that capability
33	- **Auto-filter with `hasVisual: true`** — picture mode always filters to words with visuals, no teacher toggle
34	- **No IndexedDB migration needed** — all new fields are optional, existing drafts/configs remain valid
35	- **"mum" goes in level 2** — level 2 covers graphemes `m` and `u`; needs both `CurriculumEntry` in `aus/level2.json` and `WordCore` in `core/level2.json`
36	- **Zero-results fallback to recall mode** — if `hasVisual` filtering returns nothing, fall back to audio-only rather than blank screen
37	- **Defer totalRounds silent truncation** — SRS for rounds is coming soon, making this moot
38	
39	## Spec / Plan
40	
41	- docs/brainstorms/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji-requirements.md
42	- docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-consolidate-word-emoji.md
43	
44	## Key files
45	
46	- `src/data/words/types.ts` — `CurriculumEntry`, `WordHit`, `DraftEntry`, `WordFilter` types need emoji/image fields (R1-R3, R7)
47	- `src/data/words/filter.ts:173-189` — `joinHits` must propagate emoji/image from curriculum to `WordHit` (R2)
48	- `src/data/words/filter.ts` — `draftToHit` must propagate emoji/image from `DraftEntry` (R2)
49	- `src/data/words/adapters.ts` — `toWordSpellRound` must map emoji/image through (R4)
50	- `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level1.json` — add emoji to ant, pin (R5)
51	- `src/data/words/curriculum/aus/level2.json` — add emoji to can, cat, dog, sad, sun; add mum entry (R5, R6)
52	- `src/data/words/core/level2.json` — add mum `WordCore` entry (R6)
53	- `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:83-92` — `WORD_SPELL_ROUND_POOL` to be removed (R9)
54	- `src/routes/$locale/_app/game/$gameId.tsx:286-298` — picture mode invariant must be inverted to use `source` with `hasVisual: true` (R8)
55	- `src/games/word-spell/useLibraryRounds.ts` — already maps via `toWordSpellRound`, will pick up emoji/image automatically
56

69	- `joinHits` joins curriculum entries against core entries by word — if a `WordCore` entry is missing, the curriculum entry is silently discarded. "mum" must be added to both `aus/level2.json` AND `core/level2.json`.
70	- The mode invariant in `resolveWordSpellConfig` currently _deletes_ `source` for picture mode — it must be _inverted_ so picture mode becomes source-defined with `hasVisual: true`.
